
Grok signals mostly overlap with existing README entries or lack agentic RLHF specificity. arXiv RSS yielded one strong candidate: a rubric-based reward training paper with decoupled advantage normalization directly applicable to agentic alignment. VERL is borderline but too similar to existing RLVR entry. BeSafe-Bench lacks clear RLHF component.

## Suggestions

- [Stabilizing Rubric Integration Training via Decoupled Advantage Normalization](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.26535) — Introduces decoupled advantage normalization to stabilize RL policy training when reward signals are derived from human-defined rubrics, improving alignment reliability in agentic settings.
  Section: Research Papers > Reward Modeling | Criteria: Applies reward modeling and preference learning (rubric-based rewards) in an agentic setting, Goal-directed agent behavior shaped by human-defined rubric reward signals, Iterative alignment loop where rubric integration refines agent policy, Empirical outcome: measurable training stability and alignment improvement
